QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_50

question.text: Under Regulation 21A, which of the following holdings are EXCLUDED when calculating whether key personnel of a merchant banker hold more than the permissible threshold in the issuer?

answer.text: Shares held through family trusts and HUFs
Shares held through portfolio management services
Holdings through mutual funds and ESOP
Shares pledged with banks as collateral

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 21A provides specific exclusions for holdings through mutual funds and Employee Stock Option Plans (ESOPs) when calculating whether key personnel exceed the 0.1% or Rs. 10 lakh nominal value threshold in the issuer. **The Trap:** Portfolio management services (PMS) holdings are NOT excluded — they represent direct beneficial ownership through a discretionary mandate. Family trusts and HUFs are also not excluded under this provision. **Cross-Exam Context:** MF and ESOP exclusions reflect the principle that pooled/incentive-based holdings don't create the same conflict of interest. Similar exclusions appear in SEBI insider trading regulations tested in other NISM exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check if key personnel hold issuer shares through ANY route — only MF and ESOP holdings get a pass.
